items.title: Threads, tasks and executors: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? ExecutorService automatically shuts down when submitted tasks finish, and Future.get always returns a value promptly.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Own the executor lifecycle explicitly: bound work and waiting, inspect task failure, request cancellation when needed, and shut down or close the executor.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Own the executor lifecycle explicitly: bound work and waiting, inspect task failure, request cancellation when needed, and shut down or close the executor.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.
